Role: The single web entry. Every HTTP request lands here; it boots Kirby (the CMS) which then dispatches to a template / route / panel.
PHP dependency manifest. Lists Kirby version and any other Composer packages; tells the PHP autoloader where classes live.
App semver (e.g. 0.8.46). Read by site/config/config.php and used as ?v= cache-buster on every CSS/JS include so a bump invalidates browsers automatically. Bumped on every commit.
On-disk content schema version (currently 1). Bumped only when the data shape changes incompatibly. Embedded in each snapshot's meta.json; Snapshots → Load refuses on mismatch.
Free-form working notes. Not consumed by any code.
Tells git which paths not to track — runtime artifacts (site/cache, site/sessions, media, site/accounts), the local library/* snapshot store except .gitkeep, OS junk (.DS_Store, Thumbs.db).
Git's own working data (commit history, objects, refs). Hidden from most file listings; managed entirely by git.
Public-site stylesheet. Loaded on every page. Defines design tokens (--accent: #ff5500, --text, fonts) referenced from everywhere.
Editor-only stylesheet. Loaded by site/templates/draw.php; never reaches public pages.

Runtime renderer. Reads the inlined <script id="lines-data"> payload that lines-layer.php writes, picks a viewport class, paints SVG paths, drives GSAP/ScrollTrigger animations. Loaded on every public page.
The editor itself — drawing tools, selection, group/master/palette UIs, snapshots, orphans dialog, save endpoint client. Loaded only on /dev/draw.

Phase 2 rect-block editor (vanilla JS, no build). Loaded only on /dev/page. Renders absolute-positioned rect stubs, pointer move/resize (+shift aspect-lock), numeric x/y/w/h fields, chapter management, per-rect note, save to dev/page/save.
Phase 2 /dev/page editor styling — dark dev-tool chrome mirroring /dev/draw. Kind-coloured rect stubs, selection panel, geom fields.
Phase 2 runtime stub styling for canvas-page.php. Kind colours duplicated from dev-page.css (single source of truth deferred to Slice 7).

Phase 2 /dev/image-workshop styling — shares the dark dev-tool chrome. Used by both the batch index and the comparison grid (cards, type-or-pick size combo, busy overlay, draft badge, verdict filter bar + per-card toggle + card tints).
GreenSock Animation Platform (vendored). Drives all timed/scroll animation.
GSAP plugin: lets tweens react to scroll position.
GSAP plugin: drag-and-drop primitives (currently unused but kept for future).
GSAP plugin: physics-y dragging (also unused today).
Static images that ride along with the design. Currently empty; SVG-import + image-drop flows would deposit files here.

Kirby boot config + the editor's HTTP routes (POST /dev/draw/save, dev/draw/library/{list,save,load}, dev/page/save, dev/image-workshop/save). Phase 2 added 'thumbs'=>quality 82 and the first 'hooks' block (page.create:after auto-creates a canvas-page's images child; file.update:after applies the one-time maxLongEdge cap). dev/image-workshop/save persists per-batch verdicts.json (ok/rework/dropped). Exposes option('version') and option('schemaVersion').

Host-scoped Kirby config override (e.g. config.newsitedbart.bbh.fr.php). rsync-EXCLUDED — pushed manually via SCP. Holds the ready-callback auth gate (403 for the whole /dev tree when no Panel user) + X-Powered-By strip. One file per server (each host has its own SERVER_NAME).
Kirby site-level schema (titles, etc.) used by the Kirby Panel.
Default page schema for the Kirby Panel — what fields a page in this project can have.
Phase 2 blueprint for a rect-authored page. No pageWidth field (canvas dims come from Deco's page.json). Sidebar lists the images child (image-container).
Phase 2 leaf blueprint hosting a canvas-page's image files in content/<page>/images/. Locked down (no delete/rename/template change). Files use the image template.
Phase 2 image-workshop container blueprint. Lists batch children; Panel can create them. Reached at /dev/image-workshop.
Phase 2 image-workshop batch blueprint — one triage workspace. Files use the shared image template. Grid view at /dev/image-workshop/<slug>.

Phase 2 image file blueprint (mime image/* only). Fields: alt, caption, previewLongEdge (non-destructive trial), previewInfo (link block), maxLongEdge (one-time destructive cap, applied by the file.update:after hook). NO create: transform — originals preserved.
This project's PHP plugin. Helpers like art_load_palette, art_load_class_data, art_resolve_instance, art_viewbox_attr. Bridges the on-disk content into the runtime payload format.
<head> snippet: title, viewport meta, style.css include (cache-busted).
Closing tags + app.js include.
Builds the runtime data payload (classes + palette + per-class instances/groups + page config + version) and emits the <svg id="lines-layer"> + <script id="lines-data"> blocks that app.js reads.
Page-content snippet for one of the three button shapes used in design pages.
Page-content snippet for one of the three button shapes used in design pages.
Page-content snippet for one of the three button shapes used in design pages.
Empty stub. Kirby convention: an index.html inside any sensitive directory prevents the webserver from listing it.
Fallback page template — wraps header + content + footer.
Home page template — adds the lines-layer SVG so the runtime can render on /.

The editor's template. Renders the editor toolbar + sidebar + canvas, inlines a much richer payload than the runtime (pages, classes, masters, palette, trigger suggestions, image sources, version), loads dev-draw.js. Reached at /dev/draw.
Phase 2 rect-block EDITOR template. Reads ?page=<slug>, loads that page's rects.json + Deco config for canvas dims, emits #editor-data JSON, loads dev-page.js. Reached at /dev/page.
Phase 2 RUNTIME template. Reads rects.json + Deco config, renders absolutely-positioned rect stubs at pageW × max(max(y+h),pageH)+80. Visual parity with the editor minus chrome.
Phase 2 image-workshop container index. Lists batch children (childrenAndDrafts) with links into each batch's grid. Reached at /dev/image-workshop.

Phase 2 image-workshop batch grid. Per image: original vs resize($size,$size) derivative with dims+niceSize+open links. Type-or-pick size combo (number field + disclosure caret preset menu; pick fills, Apply commits), busy overlay on regen. Step B: per-card OK/Rework/Dropped verdict toggle (persists to verdicts.json via dev/image-workshop/save), client-side filter bar, copy-rework-filenames. Reached at /dev/image-workshop/<slug>.
Where Kirby would store Panel user credentials when the Panel is in use. Contains only index.html (the directory-listing stub) — no user accounts in this project. Git ignores everything else here.
Kirby's HTTP/page cache (when enabled). Same index.html stub. Git ignores the rest.
Where Kirby keeps session files for logged-in users. Same index.html stub. Git ignores the rest.
Kirby site-level content (title, etc.) in Kirby's text format.
The class definitions: id + display name for narrow/medium/wide. Site-wide, not per-page.

The master library. Canonical visual definitions: each entry is one logical object (id, name, kind, params/points/segments/d, stroke, width, scope). Instances across every class on every page reference these.
The site-wide color palette (v4+ canonical location). Editor reads + writes here today.
v3 legacy palette file. Read only as a fallback if _shared/palette.json is missing. Safe to delete.
v1–v2 era seed palette — last-resort bootstrap for a fresh clone. Safe to delete in production.
Kirby page metadata that makes /dev/draw exist as a route — that's how the draw.php template gets reached.
Kirby page record that makes /dev/page resolve to the page.php editor template. TargetPage field names the default page to edit.
Kirby page record that makes /dev/image-workshop resolve. Batches are child pages created in the Panel under it.
Phase 2 per-page rect-block layout: { schemaVersion, chapters[], rects[] }. Authored by /dev/page, rendered by canvas-page.php. Canvas dims NOT stored here (read from Deco's page.json).
Phase 2 per-canvas-page image library (an image-container child page). Source images preserved; runtime derives thumbs lazily.
Kirby page metadata for / (home page title, etc.).
The editor's per-page config: useClasses (which classes this page uses), dims per class (pageW/H + canvasW/H).
v4+ per-class instance records. Each entry: { id, masterId, groupId, positionOffset, behaviors[], overrides }. The "skin" referencing a master. One file per class (wide, medium, narrow).
v4+ per-class group records. Each group: id, name, hidden, defaults (fallback translateX/Y, rotate, drawIn for lines in this group).
v3 legacy per-class line file. Replaced by instances.json since v4; read only if instances.json is missing. Safe to delete.
v3 legacy page-level line file (pre per-class split). Read only as fallback. Safe to delete.
v3 legacy page-level group file (pre per-class split). Read only as fallback. Safe to delete.
v1–v2 era seed file. Last-resort fallback for a fresh clone. Safe to delete in production.
v1–v2 era seed file. Last-resort fallback for a fresh clone. Safe to delete in production.
The 404 page. Same per-class structure as home/.

Schema migration CLI. Walks the content tree and ports older v1/v2/v3 shapes onto the current v4+ schema (lines.json → instances.json + masters.json, palette relocation, etc.). Runs from a terminal, not from a Kirby route.
Empty placeholder so git tracks the directory; the snapshots themselves stay local.
Snapshot metadata: { name, savedAt, appVersion, schemaVersion }.
Recursive copy of the project's content/ tree taken at snapshot time.
Directory-listing stub (Kirby's index.html convention).
(Auto-created at runtime) Resized/thumbnailed image variants Kirby generates from content/ images. Pure cache; deletable.
Kirby project's own README.
Kirby's license.
Kirby's security policy.
Kirby's contributing guide (for the upstream project).
Kirby's editor config (upstream's tab/space conventions).
The bootstrap entry that index.php includes to start Kirby.
Kirby's HTTP router shim used by some webserver setups.
TLS root certificates bundle — for outbound HTTPS requests from PHP (license validation, plugin updates if used).
Kirby's public signing key — used to verify license signatures.
Kirby's own Composer manifest.
Kirby's Composer lockfile (transitive deps pinned).
Style for the "Whoops" PHP error page (the friendly stack-trace UI shown in dev when a PHP exception escapes).

Kirby's default configuration — aliases.php, routes.php, helpers.php, tags.php, and subdirs api/, areas/, blocks/, fields/, presets/, sections/, templates/ that define the built-in Panel features. Read-only as far as this project is concerned.
Bundled PHP lib: Markdown parser.
Bundled PHP lib: Markdown extras (footnotes, tables, etc.).
Bundled PHP lib: YAML parser.
Kirby Panel UI translations (per-language JSON files).
Pluralization rules per language for the Panel UI.
Built assets (JS, CSS) for the Kirby Panel — the CMS admin UI mounted at /panel. Source not present (only the dist build).
Vestigial build-tool config from the upstream Kirby release — only relevant if you were rebuilding the Panel yourself. Inert here.
Same as above — upstream Vite config for the Panel build.
Kirby's REST API implementation.
Kirby's cache abstractions (file, APCu, Memcached, etc.).
The core CMS classes — pages, sites, files, users, languages, kirby itself.
Content reading/writing (the .txt and JSON content layer).
Data format encoders/decoders (JSON, YAML, XML).
Database abstraction (used for some Panel features).
Email sending (uses PHPMailer underneath).
Kirby's exception classes.
Form field implementations (used by the Panel and content forms).
File and directory abstractions.
Higher-level form handling (Panel form builder).
HTTP request/response, URL handling, remote requests.
Image reading + manipulation (resize/thumbnail).
Site-wide option/config handling.
The CMS Panel backend — Panel routes, areas, view definitions.
HTML parser used for content sanitization.
Plugin loading + registration system.
Query-string parser/runner for Kirby's template helpers.
Reflection helpers.
Sanitization for HTML/SVG content.
Session handling for logged-in Panel users.
Template rendering engine.
Text utilities (KirbyTags, formatting).
General-purpose helpers — Str, A (array), F (file), Url, etc.
Persistent UUIDs for pages/files/users.
Composer autoloader entry.
CLI binaries provided by Composer deps.
OTP library (two-factor auth for the Panel).
SimpleImage — image processing dep.
Composer's own runtime files.
Whoops — the friendly PHP error page library.
Laminas components (HTTP utilities).
League packages (assorted utilities).
PHP-Markdown alternative.
PHPMailer — email sending.
PSR interfaces (PHP-FIG standards).
Symfony components — HTTP, YAML, process utilities.
Internal Kirby view for the "browser too old" page.
